duration: 1 hour total between the two nights
description: The birds were heard only, on both the 22nd and 23rd.  They were first detected from approximately 960 meters (according to Google earth).  I went and got Ted and Bryan as they had gone to bed already and we walked over to confirm what I was sure I was hearing.  During the daylight hours of July 23rd I worked one of the marshes and had a bird respond with a ki-ki-krrr while also hearing another bird give some grunting/growling type calls.  The birds were found in two different marshes.  The two marshes are about 1.4 miles apart with one being entirely bull rushes and the other being almost entirely a cattail marsh.
